extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1 (IF anticoagulated), and rec_drug_b is set true only by r4 (IF anticoagulated). Both rules share the identical trigger condition (anticoagulated). When anticoagulated=true, r1 fires setting rec_drug_a=true and r4 fires setting rec_drug_b=true. There are no competing rules for these two outputs, so priorities and tie-breaking are irrelevant; both fire unconditionally. With anticoagulated=true, peptic_ulcer=false, ckd_dialysis=false: rec_drug_a=true (r1), rec_drug_b=true (r4), rec_drug_c=false (no rule fires, default). This makes rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b jointly true, violating the mutual exclusion property (no input may make both true).
